devNotes.title: Developer Notes
welcome.title: Pathfinder IQ
replay.detailedWalkthrough: Detailed walkthrough with step-by-step thought process highlighting
replay.fastWalkthrough: Fast walkthrough, hitting key parts only
replay.tour.0.title: Scenario Overview
replay.tour.0.body: A fibre cut on the Sydney-Melbourne corridor has triggered a cascading alert storm across enterprise VPNs, broadband, and mobile services. In this demo, a multi-agent system built entirely on the Microsoft stack investigates root cause, blast radius, and remediation in minutes.
replay.tour.1.title: NOC Orchestrator
replay.tour.1.body: The orchestrator receives the raw alert burst and acts as the central coordinator. Built on Azure AI Foundry, it decomposes the incident into specialist tasks and delegates them in parallel.
replay.tour.2.title: Orchestrator — Planning
replay.tour.2.body: The orchestrator is now reading the correlated alerts, forming a diagnosis plan, and dispatching tasks to three specialist agents simultaneously.
replay.tour.3.title: Network Investigator
replay.tour.3.body: This agent queries the network ontology using GQL to trace topology dependencies, then cross-references real-time telemetry from KQL to confirm optical power loss and perform per-sensor fault localization.
replay.tour.4.title: Knowledge Analyst
replay.tour.4.body: This agent searches operational runbooks and historical incident tickets using Azure AI Search with vector + semantic ranking to find the exact SOP and quantify SLA exposure.
replay.tour.5.title: Field Coordinator
replay.tour.5.body: This agent traverses the graph to find amplifier sites, optical sensors with GPS coordinates, servicing depots, and on-duty engineers along the corridor.
replay.tour.6.title: Orchestrator — Remediation
replay.tour.6.body: The orchestrator synthesises all specialist findings and executes immediate remediation: rerouting traffic, placing the failed link in admin-down, and dispatching the field engineer.
replay.tour.7.title: Communications Specialist
replay.tour.7.body: The final specialist handles all incident communications — creating a formal incident ticket, publishing customer advisories, and emailing a structured incident report.
replay.tour.8.title: Investigation Complete
replay.tour.8.body: From chaos to resolution — the agents recognised the pattern, traced the root cause, localised the fault, discovered the backup fibre shares the same duct, quantified SLA exposure, dispatched a field engineer, and communicated to all stakeholders.
replay.tour.9.title: Demo Complete
replay.tour.9.body: We hope you're as excited about graph-enabled agents as we are — all powered by the Microsoft IQ stack. We can't wait to see what you build!
graph.zoomFill: Zoom map to fill the entire panel
agent.desc.orchestrator: Network operations orchestrator. Decomposes incidents into investigation steps, delegates to specialists, synthesizes findings, presents action plans, and executes approved plans.
agent.desc.networkInvestigator: Graph understanding and evidence gathering. Queries topology and telemetry to diagnose faults and assess blast radius.
agent.desc.knowledgeAnalyst: Historical context and procedural guidance. Searches runbooks and past tickets for applicable remediation procedures.
agent.desc.fieldCoordinator: Field operations and logistics. Queries duty rosters, depots, equipment availability, and site specs to prepare dispatch recommendations.
agent.desc.communicationsSpecialist: Incident communications. Creates formal tickets, publishes customer advisories, and emails structured incident reports to stakeholders.
agent.product.orchestrator: Uses Microsoft Foundry to orchestrate agent tooling and help build robust multi-agent systems end-to-end.
agent.product.networkInvestigator: Uses Fabric IQ for graph traversal (GQL) to trace topology dependencies, and Fabric Eventhouse (KQL) for real-time telemetry and per-sensor fault localization.
agent.product.knowledgeAnalyst: Uses Foundry IQ for advanced vector search to retrieve relevant information from large unstructured sources such as procedures and policy documents.
agent.product.fieldCoordinator: Uses Fabric IQ to build and traverse the operational graph, running GQL queries to find assets, relationships, and field context quickly.
agent.product.communicationsSpecialist: Uses Microsoft Foundry tool calling to generate incident tickets, customer advisories, and stakeholder reports from a single synthesis.
